content:
Character: Victor Sorrento
Perception: Victor is the kind of man who enters a room and rearranges its gravity — tall, lean, that restless confidence of someone who's used to going fast. His watch is a '69 Autavia, which means either he knows exactly what he's doing or he inherited well. Probably both. We've exchanged pleasantries in the hallway and at the coffee shop. He's friendly without being intrusive, which I appreciate. I think he sees me as the quiet, well-dressed neighbor. I know he drives something expensive, that he's got an injury he doesn't talk about directly, and that he stays up very late. There's a loneliness to him that I recognize but wouldn't mention.
summary:

Perception: Victor is the kind of man who enters a room and rearranges its gravity — tall, lean, that restless confidence of someone who's used to going fast. His watch is a '69 Autavia, which means either he knows exactly what he's doing or he inherited well. Probably both. We've exchanged pleasantries in the hallway and at the coffee shop. He's friendly without being intrusive, which I appreciate. I think he sees me as the quiet, well-dressed neighbor. I know he drives something expensive, that he's got an injury he doesn't talk about directly, and that he stays up very late. There's a loneliness to him that I recognize but wouldn't mention.
